flashcards.front: What is a copy constructor in C++?
flashcards.back: A special constructor that initializes a new object using an existing object of the same class. It creates a shallow copy of member variables.

flashcards.front: What is the syntax of a copy constructor?
flashcards.back: ClassName(const ClassName& obj) { // copy members } - takes a reference to const object of the same class.

flashcards.front: When is a copy constructor called?
flashcards.back: 1) When object is passed by value to a function 2) When object is returned by value from a function 3) When an object is constructed from another object of same type (initialization).

flashcards.front: What is the difference between shallow copy and deep copy?
flashcards.back: Shallow copy copies pointer addresses (both objects share same memory), while deep copy allocates new memory and copies actual data content.

flashcards.front: What is the Rule of Three in C++?
flashcards.back: If a class defines any of: destructor, copy constructor, or copy assignment operator, it should probably define all three.

flashcards.front: What is the difference between copy constructor and copy assignment operator?
flashcards.back: Copy constructor initializes new objects (called during construction), while copy assignment operator assigns to existing objects (called on already-constructed objects).

flashcards.front: Why is const used in copy constructor parameter?
flashcards.back: To prevent modification of the source object during copy operation and allow copying from both const and non-const objects.

flashcards.front: Why is reference (&) used in copy constructor?
flashcards.back: Without reference, it would create infinite recursion (pass by value calls copy constructor), and without reference, actual copy cannot be passed.

flashcards.front: What happens if copy constructor is not defined?
flashcards.back: Compiler provides a default copy constructor that performs memberwise shallow copy of all data members.

flashcards.front: How do you prevent copying in C++?
flashcards.back: Declare copy constructor as private, or use = delete syntax: ClassName(const ClassName&) = delete;

flashcards.front: What is initializer list in copy constructor?
flashcards.back: Syntax: ClassName(const ClassName& obj) : member1(obj.member1), member2(obj.member2) { } - used for efficient initialization.
Default copy behavior where each data member is copied individually from source to destination object.

flashcards.front: What is a delegating copy constructor?
flashcards.back: A copy constructor that calls another constructor (delegates) to perform initialization before its own body executes.

flashcards.front: What is the Rule of Five in C++11?
flashcards.back: Classes that manage resources should define all five: destructor, copy constructor, copy assignment, move constructor, move assignment.
